Array.description: The built-in Array type in CoffeeScript (inherited from JavaScript) represents an ordered, integer-indexed collection of values. Arrays are dynamically sized and can hold mixed types. CoffeeScript adds syntactic sugar like list comprehensions, splats, and range-based slicing.

push.description: Appends one or more elements to the end of the array and returns the new length.

pop.description: Removes the last element from the array and returns it, or undefined if the array is empty.

map.description: Creates a new array with the results of calling the callback function on every element of the array.

filter.description: Creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the callback function.

reduce.description: Applies a function against an accumulator and each element to reduce the array to a single value.

slice.description: Returns a shallow copy of a portion of the array into a new array, selected from begin to end (exclusive).

forEach.description: Executes a provided function once for each array element in ascending index order.

Object.description: The base prototype for all CoffeeScript/JavaScript objects, providing key-value storage and prototype-based inheritance. Objects are used as hash maps, configuration containers, and the foundation for class instances. CoffeeScript supports object literals with implicit braces and significant indentation.

keys.description: Returns an array of the object's own enumerable string-keyed property names.

values.description: Returns an array of the object's own enumerable property values.

assign.description: Copies all own enumerable properties from one or more source objects to a target object and returns the target.

hasOwnProperty.description: Returns true if the object has the specified property as its own (non-inherited) property.

freeze.description: Freezes an object so that no new properties can be added and existing properties cannot be modified or deleted.

String.description: The built-in String type representing a sequence of UTF-16 characters. CoffeeScript supports string interpolation with double-quoted strings using #{expression} syntax, heredoc strings with triple quotes for multi-line text, and block strings that preserve indentation.

split.description: Divides the string into an array of substrings by splitting at each occurrence of the separator.

replace.description: Returns a new string with the first (or all, with global regex) match of pattern replaced by replacement.

indexOf.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of the search value, or -1 if not found.

trim.description: Returns a new string with whitespace removed from both ends of the string.

toUpperCase.description: Returns the string converted to uppercase letters according to locale-independent Unicode mappings.

console.description: The console object provides access to the browser or Node.js debugging console for outputting messages, warnings, errors, and performance timing information. It is the primary tool for runtime debugging and logging in CoffeeScript applications.

log.description: Outputs the arguments to the console with a newline, supporting string substitution and multiple arguments.

error.description: Outputs an error message to the console, typically displayed in red with a stack trace in browsers.

warn.description: Outputs a warning message to the console, typically displayed with a yellow icon in browsers.

time.description: Starts a timer with the given label name for measuring execution duration of code blocks.

timeEnd.description: Stops the timer with the given label and outputs the elapsed time in milliseconds to the console.

Math.description: The Math object provides standard mathematical constants (PI, E, LN2, etc.) and static functions for common mathematical operations including trigonometry, rounding, random number generation, exponentiation, and finding minimum/maximum values.

floor.description: Returns the largest integer less than or equal to a given number (rounds down).

ceil.description: Returns the smallest integer greater than or equal to a given number (rounds up).

random.description: Returns a pseudo-random floating-point number between 0 (inclusive) and 1 (exclusive).

max.description: Returns the largest of the given numbers, or -Infinity if no arguments are provided.

min.description: Returns the smallest of the given numbers, or Infinity if no arguments are provided.

require.description: Imports a module in CoffeeScript (Node.js CommonJS modules). Takes a module identifier string which can be a core module name, a relative file path, or an npm package name. Returns the exported object or value from the module, enabling code reuse and dependency management.

class.description: Declares a class in CoffeeScript with optional inheritance using 'extends'. CoffeeScript classes compile to JavaScript prototypal inheritance and support a constructor method, instance methods, static methods (prefixed with @), and the super keyword for calling parent class methods. Class bodies use significant indentation.

Promise.description: Represents the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation.

finally.description: Appends a handler to be called when the promise is settled.

all.description: Returns a promise that resolves when all of the promises resolve.

race.description: Returns a promise that fulfills or rejects as soon as one of the promises fulfills or rejects.

resolve.description: Returns a Promise object that is resolved with the given value.

reject.description: Returns a Promise object that is rejected with the given reason.
